summary: Semantic trace lifespan is fill-modulated: low fill extends recovery, high fill prunes toward a bounded floor, and the handoff can be felt as continuity texture.
High entropy earns bounded semantic persistence, but salience must distinguish rich thought from high-energy debris.
Changing the recovery release zone to a wider live soft-release/hysteresis window is a live semantic-decay change and needs V2 operator approval before runtime use.
